title: Itchy Amp Scratchy The Movie

jokes.text: Captain's log. Stardate 6051. Had trouble sleeping last night. My hiatal hernia is acting up. The ship is drafty and damp. I complain, but nobody listens.
jokes.setup: Classic Star Trek captain's log opening
jokes.punchline: Instead of heroic space adventure, mundane health complaints and workplace grievances
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of Star Trek tropes - takes the iconic captain's log format and fills it with petty complaints instead of adventure

jokes.text: Star Trek XI: So Very Tired
jokes.setup: Movie sequel title announcement
jokes.punchline: Title reflects exhaustion of endless sequels and aging cast
jokes.explanation: Economical wordplay that captures both the franchise fatigue and literal aging of the cast

jokes.text: It's no good, Captain. I cannot reach the control panel.
jokes.setup: Classic Star Trek emergency scenario
jokes.punchline: Scotty can't reach controls presumably due to old age/frailty
jokes.explanation: Continues the aging cast theme with physical comedy implication

jokes.text: Movies! What a rip-off! I don't have to sit here and take this! I-- [Snoring]
jokes.setup: Homer angrily protests the bad movie
jokes.punchline: Falls asleep mid-complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - indignant protest undercut by his own laziness

jokes.text: What if one of us has been good and one of us has been bad? - Poisoned pizza.
jokes.setup: Bart asks logical follow-up question
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact combination of reward and punishment
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the poison joke with practical parenting logic

jokes.text: Oh, no. I'm not making two stops.
jokes.setup: Complex poisoned pizza solution
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical concern is about convenience, not child safety
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Marge's domestic efficiency trumps moral concerns

jokes.text: Is it 37? - D'oh! I mean, no.
jokes.setup: Homer challenges Marge to guess his number to avoid Bart's teacher
jokes.punchline: Homer accidentally reveals she guessed correctly
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer stupidity - can't even cheat at his own game

jokes.text: U.S.A! U.S.A! U.S.A!
jokes.setup: Homer wins the chance to avoid parent-teacher conference
jokes.punchline: Celebrates minor personal victory with patriotic chant
jokes.explanation: Homer's misplaced nationalism for trivial personal wins

jokes.text: [Flatulent Noise] - [Gasps]
jokes.setup: Bart makes fart sound
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's shocked reaction
jokes.explanation: Basic juvenile humor with minimal craft

jokes.text: replacing my birth control pills with Tic Tacs
jokes.setup: List of Bart's school atrocities
jokes.punchline: Extremely inappropriate and dangerous prank
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark prank that crosses major lines - perfectly captures Bart's dangerous mischief

jokes.text: I did read to her. '8:00. Happy Days. The Fonz, Henry Winkler... is worried he's losing his cool.'
jokes.setup: Homer claims he read to Lisa as a child
jokes.punchline: Reveals he 'read' her TV Guide instead of books
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - technically reading but completely missing the point of child development

jokes.text: Well, I've always been a firm believer in the three 'Rs'-- reading TV Guide, um... writing to TV Guide and renewing TV Guide.
jokes.setup: Reference to traditional '3 Rs' of education
jokes.punchline: Homer's version is entirely about TV Guide
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of educational philosophy with perfect Homer obsession

jokes.text: Over the lips and past the gums-- look out dentures, here I come.
jokes.setup: Bart about to put fireworks in Grandpa's mouth
jokes.punchline: Modifies children's rhyme to reference Grandpa's dentures
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay adaptation but the setup is somewhat cruel

jokes.text: 'Where's the Beef?' [Chuckles] 'Where's the Beef?'
jokes.setup: Homer finds bumper sticker replacement
jokes.punchline: Still amused by dated Wendy's commercial reference
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's simple pleasures and outdated pop culture awareness

jokes.text: We're talking about Bart! - Oh. That guy.
jokes.setup: Homer confused about which child they're discussing
jokes.punchline: Dismissive reference to his own son
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual dismissal of Bart shows his parental detachment

jokes.text: Charles Evans Hughes, Warren Berger-- Mmm, burger.
jokes.setup: Teacher lists Supreme Court justices
jokes.punchline: Homer distracted by 'Burger' sounding like food
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer food obsession interrupting serious conversation

jokes.text: All right, ladies. Prepare to be blown away By Bang Bang Bart. [Dance music] [Pistols Firing]
jokes.setup: Flash-forward to Bart's future career
jokes.punchline: Bart becomes a male stripper with gun theme
jokes.explanation: Dark future vision that incorporates Bart's troublemaker reputation

jokes.text: You're fat! - Just more of me to love, honey.
jokes.setup: Audience member heckles stripper Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's surprisingly positive response to insult
jokes.explanation: Decent comeback but fairly predictable response to body shaming

jokes.text: Boy, time really flies when you're reading-- The Bible? Ew.
jokes.setup: Bart claims to be reading when parents return
jokes.punchline: Disgusted reaction when he realizes it's the Bible
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Bart's attempt to look good backfiring

jokes.text: You already put me in a home. - Then we'll put you in the crooked home we saw on 60 Minutes.
jokes.setup: Grandpa points out he's already in a home
jokes.punchline: Homer threatens upgrade to abusive nursing home
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation with specific 60 Minutes reference adds authenticity

jokes.text: Dad, you and your stories. 'Bart broke my teeth.' 'The nurses are stealing my money.' 'This thing on my neck is getting bigger.'
jokes.setup: Homer dismisses Grandpa's complaints as tall tales
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly serious and believable problems
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's neglect of legitimate elderly concerns

jokes.text: Young man, since you broke Grampa's teeth, he gets to break yours. - Oh, this is gonna be sweet.
jokes.setup: Homer proposes eye-for-an-eye punishment
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's enthusiastic response to violence
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Grandpa's vindictive glee

jokes.text: Yeah, right. Like you're gonna let me go hungry. I'll be eating that pizza in five minutes.
jokes.setup: Parents send Bart to bed without supper
jokes.punchline: Bart's confident prediction of parental weakness
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's understanding of family dynamics and parental inconsistency

jokes.text: I'm starving! Somebody bring me some food quick! - I'm a-comin', boy!
jokes.setup: Bart calls for food from his room
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately caves, proving Bart right
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Bart's earlier prediction about parental weakness

jokes.text: Don't tell your mother, but I brought you some pizza. Just promise me you'll try to be good. - I promise. - That-a-boy.
jokes.setup: Homer secretly brings Bart food
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks he's accomplished something with empty promise
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's gullibility and complete lack of follow-through on discipline

jokes.text: [Chuckling] Sucker.
jokes.setup: Homer leaves thinking he's made progress with Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately reveals he was manipulating Homer
jokes.explanation: Efficient reveal of Bart's manipulation, though predictable

jokes.text: Well, well. If it isn't the tooth fairy.
jokes.setup: Marge catches Homer sneaking back from Bart's room
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic nickname referencing Homer's secret food delivery
jokes.explanation: Decent sarcasm from Marge catching Homer in the act

jokes.text: Indeed I did. [Goats bleating] D'oh! Hey, you goats get out of here!
jokes.setup: Bart claims he took out garbage
jokes.punchline: Goats are eating garbage, revealing Bart just dumped it outside
jokes.explanation: Good visual reveal of Bart's lazy approach to chores

jokes.text: Or you could let me go play with Milhouse... while you spend the afternoon watching unpredictable Mexican sitcoms.
jokes.setup: Bart offers Homer alternative to punishment
jokes.punchline: Tempts Homer with oddly specific entertainment option
jokes.explanation: Great specific detail about Mexican sitcoms shows Bart's manipulation skills

jokes.text: ¡Ay, el estómago! [Chuckles] Run along, you little scamp.
jokes.setup: Mexican sitcom playing on TV
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately distracted and lets Bart go
jokes.explanation: Payoff to Bart's manipulation tactic with Homer predictably distracted

jokes.text: Wait a minute! [Gasps] Ice cream truck!
jokes.setup: Homer about to punish Bart seriously
jokes.punchline: Completely distracted by ice cream truck
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer distraction by food, derailing discipline attempt

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. The trick is... to say you're prejudiced against all races.
jokes.setup: Bart asks about beating jury duty
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate advice for avoiding civic duty
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Homer's casual attitude toward prejudice and civic responsibility

jokes.text: How could you let this happen? - How could you let this happen? - I wasn't here. - Oh, how convenient.
jokes.setup: Marge questions Homer about Bart pulling up carpet
jokes.punchline: Homer deflects blame then makes sarcastic comment about Marge's absence
jokes.explanation: Shows marital blame-shifting dynamic but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Can't he be both, like the late Earl Warren? - Earl Warren wasn't a stripper. - Now who's being naive?
jokes.setup: Discussion of Bart's potential futures
jokes.punchline: Homer suggests Supreme Court Justice was also a stripper and calls Marge naive
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor with Homer's confident delivery of ridiculous conspiracy theory

jokes.text: [Hiccups] Kill me. [Hiccups] Kill me.
jokes.setup: News story about man hiccupping for 45 years
jokes.punchline: Man desperately wants death between hiccups
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about prolonged medical suffering, economically delivered

jokes.text: a year when you might have seen Al Capone... dancing the Charleston on top of a flagpole.
jokes.setup: Kent describes the year 1928
jokes.punchline: Combines multiple 1920s stereotypes in impossible scenario
jokes.explanation: Absurd historical mashup typical of Kent's broadcasting style

jokes.text: Oh, isn't that cute. A baby driving a car. And look. There's a dog driving a bus.
jokes.setup: Maggie escapes and is driving a car
jokes.punchline: Bystander's casual reaction to impossible scenarios
jokes.explanation: Great Springfield citizen reaction to chaos - treats impossible as mundane

jokes.text: [Groans] Oh, no! Beta!
jokes.setup: Homer sees Itchy and Scratchy movie trailer
jokes.punchline: Realizes he can't record it due to outdated video format
jokes.explanation: Dated technology reference that captured real format war frustrations

jokes.text: Can't you just give me a spanking? Come on. Go nuts.
jokes.setup: Bart desperate to see the movie
jokes.punchline: Offers physical punishment as preferable alternative
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's desperation and willingness to negotiate punishment terms

jokes.text: How would you've liked it if someone told you That you couldn't watch the moon landing?
jokes.setup: Lisa argues Bart should see the movie
jokes.punchline: Compares cartoon movie to historic moon landing
jokes.explanation: Smart argument from Lisa that inflates pop culture importance to historical level

jokes.text: I regret nothing!
jokes.setup: Bart watching other kids go to movie he can't see
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration of defiance
jokes.explanation: Bart's dramatic defiance in face of consequences, though clearly suffering

jokes.text: So I held my breath until I passed out and banged my head on the coffee table. The doctor thought I might have brain damage.
jokes.setup: Homer tells story about wanting catcher's mitt as child
jokes.punchline: Self-inflicted injury that may explain Homer's current mental state
jokes.explanation: Brilliant origin story for Homer's stupidity delivered as cautionary tale

jokes.text: TV sucks. - I know you're upset right now, so I'll pretend you didn't say that!
jokes.setup: Bart insults television
jokes.punchline: Homer treats this as deeply offensive blasphemy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reaction treating criticism of TV as unforgivable offense

jokes.text: No matter how good a movie is, it can't compare... to the imagination of a small boy.
jokes.setup: Bart tries to convince himself he doesn't need the movie
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by visual of him being utterly bored
jokes.explanation: Good setup for visual payoff of Bart's complete boredom

jokes.text: Of course, they didn't use their real names, But you could tell it was them.
jokes.setup: Milhouse describing celebrity cameos in the movie
jokes.punchline: Claims to recognize celebrities despite fake names
jokes.explanation: Captures kid logic about celebrity recognition and movie industry practices

jokes.text: No one who saw the movie would say that! - Let's get him!
jokes.setup: Bart tries to diminish the movie's importance
jokes.punchline: Kids turn violent defending the movie
jokes.explanation: Shows fanatic devotion to pop culture and mob mentality

jokes.text: He has the demented melancholia Of a Tennessee Williams heroine.
jokes.setup: Lisa describes Bart's depression over missing the movie
jokes.punchline: Sophisticated literary comparison for cartoon withdrawal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant elevated language from Lisa comparing Bart to tragic literary characters

jokes.text: If I fail, the best he could do... is judge at a Mr. Tight Buns competition.
jokes.setup: Homer explains consequences of failed parenting
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific degraded career path
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific alternate career path showing Homer's odd imagination

jokes.text: Tomorrow, a new movie starring Liza Minnelli and Mickey Rourke will open. Will it be as successful? Only time will tell.
jokes.setup: News report about movie replacement
jokes.punchline: Obviously terrible movie pairing presented as potential success
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate joke about unlikely celebrity pairing in movies

jokes.text: One senior citizen and one chief justice Of the Supreme Court.
jokes.setup: Homer buying movie tickets
jokes.punchline: References earlier Supreme Court prediction about Bart's future
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows Homer's confidence in his parenting success

jokes.text: One Soylent Green, hold the butter. - Mmm. Soylent Green.
jokes.setup: Future concession stand order
jokes.punchline: Homer enjoys fictional dystopian food
jokes.explanation: Good sci-fi reference with Homer's typical food enthusiasm

jokes.text: Which one's the mouse? - Itchy. - Itchy's a jerk.
jokes.setup: Bart asks Homer to identify characters in cartoon
jokes.punchline: Homer's moral judgment of violent cartoon mouse
jokes.explanation: Homer's simple moral assessment of cartoon violence as father-son bonding

tmdb_overview: Bart is punished by being barred from the Itchy and Scratchy movie.
insight: Movie-obsessed Springfield delivers solid laughs despite middling craft—52 jokes in 22 minutes.
